Preeti Choudhary.review_text: This was the hardest class I've taken in Eller. Pair that with a teacher who writes test that are purposefully tough and has an iffy attitude, and you've got yourself a doozy. Preeti is often condescending, and prepare to study 8+ hours for each midterm. If possible, take this one with another professor. Ridiculous.

Choudhary is... not good. The upside is that she seems to hate all of her students equally, which eases the pain of being treated with a sort of detached contempt each time you ask her a question. No advice but to weather the storm if you're in one of her classes, it can only get better.

I have never written a review for a professor, I felt it was necessary for Professor Choudhary. I loved accounting until her class. I understood the material so well but would fail tests because of the obscure questions. The homework website is AWFUL and not user friendly at all. It was far more confusing than helpful. DO NOT take her class!

1. Prof. Choudhary is a kind and experienced woman. she can be a bit hard to follow.
2. If you show up to class, study a few days before the exams, attend office hours as needed, and have half a brain, you'll achieve a B(+). Not sure if finance is for you, otherwise. 
3. 50% was curved to a 78% on the final. She gives plenty of partial credit.

The content is already difficult but she makes it more difficult Exams cover about 30% of the practice exam, homework, and notes. A cheat sheet is allowed but not helpful since you base it on the practice exam. She is often rude and gets frustrated if you ask for clarification more than once. If possible, take this course with another professor.

Preeti is unfair when it comes to attendance, with certain days netting more attendance days than others, like making days on the week of school breaks more points which is completely unfair for out of state students. Exams are very difficult and she moves very fast. She is clearly an expert, but it was a negative experience as a student overall.

Dont take ACCT 400C with Prof. Choudhary if all your ACCT courses have been online. Was her first time teaching the class, she definitely knows her stuff, but when it comes to teaching 2 years worth of content its hell. Used to have a 4.0 GPA, offered tons of extra credit, that didnt help. Resources dont help. Doesnt consider student feedback.
Overall a horrible experience. Preeti is dismissive and rude a majority of the time. Tests are far harder than in class content. If you are able to avoid her, absolutely do it.

Class is always hard to follow. She makes tons of mistakes on her slides and clicker questions. The exams are unreasonably difficult and very different from the questions we do in class. This class made me hate accounting.

This class was so difficult and the exams were nothing like what you learn in class. This class was already extremely difficult, but I dont feel like I was actually taught anything of value. Try to get another professor if you want to keep your GPA up.

She lives up to her negative reviews. All around bad teacher who brings an attitude and gives very hard exams. Exam content is way harder than anything discussed in class. She was the only option available but absolutely take someone else if you can.

Poor teacher. Slides and lectures do not correlate at all to what you are tested on. Test material is significantly more difficult than homework or in class work. She does not explain things well and has an attitude when you ask questions. Would not recommend at all.

She knows her acct knowledge fs, but that doesn't mean she knows how to teach. Class has a lot of content so you do move fast, but her notes are all over the place and hard to learn from. Like any acct course def requires lots of attention. She does have favorites and is sassy towards some students. Raise your hand in class, has extra points.

Hopefully she reads this, as she is a very caring and kind professor - her tests are just horrible.

She gives students a studyguide that had literally (seriously) zero questions on the tests. She counteracts this by giving you a cheatsheet, but since you think the studyguide will be like the test, you base your cheatsheet on it, making it useless.

I don't usually write these, but I don't feel these reviews are fair. Content accounting majors cover in two semester is squished into this single-semester course. We learn a lot very quickly. Choudary will give you everything you need to succeed, but you have to really prioritize this course. Take advantage of homework sessions, do all EC, & STUDY

Ann Mcgrath.review_text: She's very tough. Provides no study guides at all. Her attendance quizzes are very hard and you will often miss points on them and it is as if you didn't go to class. The tests are extremely difficult given that you basically have to read over every topic due to her minimal description of what is on the test. A very difficult class.

This Accounting class was terrible. The teaching itself isn't bad, the way she conducts the class is poor. Every day, an in-class participation quiz is difficult (she tracks your location, attempting these at home is not an option). She provides no study guides, only vague 'concepts'. if you get on her bad side, she can be very disrespectful.

Professor McGrath is probably the best accounting teacher I've had. She does well to keep the class engaged and is very funny in lectures. There are attendance quizzes every single class which can be kind of difficult. Very very easy to get a B in the class with how many points are available, but more challenging to get an A.

Rick Mergenthaler.review_text: Extremely nice guy, really cares about his students. Lectures are pointless you have to learn everything on your own by going to office hours or looking through notes. Lets you use a notecard on tests but puts financial statements on tests and doesnt teach you how to read them. Extremely large curve.

Though this class was very challenging, a lot of our exams and quizzes were curved. He is a very caring professor. He wants every one of his students to succeed so he makes himself available as much as he can. I would recommend this class to any student, but be ready for a challenge. It is possible to succeed in his class.

He is a great person but not great at teaching. He goes to fast and expects you to know the information before coming to class. He goes off on personal tangents a lot. If you are like me, accounting doesn't come easy, so this class was hard. I felt like I was drowning & not learning anything. Tip: find classmates that get it and have them help you

Lin Cheng.review_text: In fact, this is a tough class. But, Professor Lin will give you huge curve for each exam. Just go to class and take notes, you will be fine. Overall, he is a very nice instructor, and he really wants to help students be successful in Accounting.

Great professor. He made the extra effort to help students inside and outside of lectures. Tests are difficult, and the course lacked a true variety and depth of practice problems to help prepare you for the exam. However, as long as you understand the problems conceptually, you should be fine

Mr. Cheng is a great teacher.  Incredibly nice and always willing to help students and answer questions.  However, the class is very difficult because of the material.  I highly recommend keeping up with the readings if possible.

Lin Cheng is an amazing teacher. Accounting is hard but Lin makes it better by explaining problems step by step. He also curves his tests depending on how well everyone does. He really cares about his students and wants everyone to succeed and get A's.

Professor Cheng was my favorite teacher this semester. He cares about all his students and wants them to succeed and get A's. Do not get me wrong, the material is not easy. If you go to his office hours and show him that you want to succeed, he will help you. He is a great guy.

Richard Mergenthaler.review_text: Extremely nice guy, really cares about his students. Lectures are pointless you have to learn everything on your own by going to office hours or looking through notes. Lets you use a notecard on tests but puts financial statements on tests and doesnt teach you how to read them. Extremely large curve.

Though this class was very challenging, a lot of our exams and quizzes were curved. He is a very caring professor. He wants every one of his students to succeed so he makes himself available as much as he can. I would recommend this class to any student, but be ready for a challenge. It is possible to succeed in his class.

He is a great person but not great at teaching. He goes to fast and expects you to know the information before coming to class. He goes off on personal tangents a lot. If you are like me, accounting doesn't come easy, so this class was hard. I felt like I was drowning & not learning anything. Tip: find classmates that get it and have them help you
